source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this Psalm the title is: “At the end, in hymns, understanding to David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What the “end” is, we will briefly call to your recollection, because ye have known it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the end of the Law is Christ, for righteousness unto every man believing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be the attention therefore directed unto the End, directed unto Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore is He called the end?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because whatever we do, to Him we refer it, and when to Him we shall have come home, more to ask we shall not have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is an end spoken of which doth consume, there is an end spoken of which doth make perfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In one sense, for instance, we understand it, when we hear, there is ended the food which was in eating; and in another sense we understand it when we hear, there is ended the vesture which was in weaving: in each case we hear, there is ended; but the food so that it no longer is, the vesture so that it is perfected.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our end therefore ought to be our perfection, our perfection Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in Him we are made perfect, because of Himself the Head, the Members are we.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he hath been spoken of as “the End of the Law,” because without Him no one doth make perfect the Law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore ye hear in the Psalms, “At the end,”—for many Psalms are thus superscribed,—be not your thought upon consuming, but upon consummation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In hymns:” in praises.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whether we are troubled and are straitened, or whether we rejoice and exult, He is to be praised, who both in tribulations doth instruct, and in gladness doth comfort.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the praise of God from the heart and mouth of a Christian man ought not to depart; not that he may be praising in prosperity, and speaking evil in adversity; but after the manner that this Psalm doth prescribe, “I will speak good of the Lord in every time, alway the praise of Him is in my mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost rejoice; acknowledge a Father indulging: thou art troubled; acknowledge a Father chastening.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whether He indulge, or whether He chasten, He is instructing one for whom He is preparing an inheritance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is, “Understanding to David himself”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: David indeed was, as we know, a holy prophet, king of Israel, son of Jesse: but because out of his seed there came for our salvation after the flesh the Lord Jesus Christ, often under that name He is figured, and David instead of Christ is in a figure set down, because of the origin of the Flesh of the Same.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For after some sort He is Son of David, after some sort He is the Lord of David; Son of David after the flesh, Lord of David after the divinity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if by Him have been made all things, by Him also David himself hath been made, out of whose seed He came to men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover, when the Lord had questioned the Jews, whose Son they affirmed Christ to be, they made answer, “David’s:” where the Lord chides the Jews, when they said that He was the Son of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saw that they had stayed at the flesh, and had lost sight of the divinity; and He reproveth them by propounding a question: “How then doth David himself in spirit call Him Lord, ‘The Lord hath said unto my Lord.’…If then He in spirit calleth Him Lord, how is He is Son?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A question He propounded; His being Son He denied not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard “Lord;” say ye how He is his “Son:” ye have heard “Son;” say how He is “Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This question the Catholic Faith solveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then David in a figure is Christ, but Christ, as we have often reminded your Love, is both Head and Body; neither ought we to speak of ourselves as alien from Christ, of whom we are members, nor to count ourselves as if we were any other thing: because “The two shall be in one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This is a great Sacrament,” saith the Apostle, “but I speak in regard of Christ and the Church.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then whole Christ is “Head and Body;” when we hear, “Understanding to David himself,” understand we ourselves also in David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the members of Christ understand, and Christ in His members understand, and the members of Christ in Christ understand: because Head and Members are one Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Head was in heaven, and was saying, “Why dost thou persecute Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We with Him are in heaven through hope, Himself is with us on earth through love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “understanding to David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be we admonished when we hear, and let the Church understand: for there belongeth to us great diligence to understand in what evil we now are, and from what evil we desire to be delivered, remembering the Prayer of the Lord, where at the end we say, “Deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore amid many tribulations of this world, this Psalm complaineth somewhat of understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He lamenteth not with it, who hath not understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But furthermore, dearly beloved, we ought to remember, that after the image of God we have been made, and that not in any other part than in the understanding itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in many things by beasts we are surpassed: but when a man knoweth himself to have been made after the image of God, therein something in himself he acknowledgeth to be more than hath been given to dumb animals.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But on consideration of all those things which a man hath, he findeth himself in this thing peculiarly distinguished from a dumb animal, in that he hath himself an understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence certain men despising in themselves that peculiar and especial thing which from their Maker they had received, the Maker Himself reproveth, saying, “Do not become like horse and mule, in which there is no understanding.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear Thou, O God, my entreaty, and despise not my prayer: give heed unto me, and hearken unto me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of one earnest, anxious, of one set in tribulation, are these words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is praying, suffering many things, from evil yearning to be delivered: it remaineth that we hear in what evil he is, and when he beginneth to speak, let us acknowledge there ourselves to be; in order that the tribulation being shared, we may conjoin prayer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been made sad in my exercise, and have been troubled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where made sad, where troubled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In my exercise,” he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of evil men, whom he suffereth, he hath made mention, and the same suffering of evil men he hath called his “exercise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think ye not that without profit there are evil men in this world, and that no good God maketh of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every evil man either on this account liveth that he may be corrected, or on this account liveth that through him a good man may be exercised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O that therefore they that do now exercise us would be converted, and together with us be exercised!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, so long as they are such as to exercise, let us not hate them: because in that wherein any one of them is evil, whether unto the end he is to persevere, we know not; and ofttimes when to thyself thou seemest to have been hating an enemy, thou hast been hating a brother, and knowest not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil and his angels in the holy Scriptures have been manifested to us, that for fire everlasting they have been destined.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of them only must amendment be despaired of.…Therefore since this rule of Love for thee is fixed, that imitating the Father thou shouldest love an enemy: for, He saith, “love your enemies:” in this precept how wouldest thou be exercised, if thou hadst no enemy to suffer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seest then that he profiteth thee somewhat: and let God sparing evil men profit thee, so that thou show mercy: because perchance thou too, if thou art a good man, out of an evil man hast been made a good man: and if God spared not evil men, not even thou wouldest be found to return thanks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May He therefore spare others, that hath spared thee also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it were not right, when thou hadst passed through, to close up the way of godliness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then doth this man pray, set among evil men, with whose enmities he was being exercised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why saith he, “I have been made sad in my exercise, and have been troubled”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While he is extending his love so as to love enemies, he hath been affected with disgust, being bayed at all around by the enmities of many men, by the frenzy of many and under a sort of human infirmity he hath sunk.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath seen himself now begin to be pierced through with an evil suggestion of the devil, to bring on hatred against his enemies: wrestling against hatred in order to perfect love herself, in the very fight, and in the wrestling, he hath been troubled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is his voice in another Psalm, “Mine eye hath been troubled, because of anger.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what followeth there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have waxen old among all mine enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if in storm and waves he were beginning to sink, like Peter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he doth trample the waves of this world, that loveth enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ on the sea was walking fearless, from whose heart there could not by any means be taken away the love of an enemy, who hanging on the Cross did say, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peter too would walk.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He as Head, Peter as Body: because, “Upon this rock,” He saith, “I will build My Church.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was bidden to walk, and he was walking by the Grace of Him bidding, not by his own strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when he saw the wind mighty, he feared; and then he began to sink, being troubled in his exercise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By what mighty wind?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By the voice of the enemy, and by the tribulation of the sinner” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, in the same manner as he cried out on the waves, “Lord, I perish, save me,” a similar voice from this man hath preceded, “Hearken unto me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what sufferest thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what dost thou groan?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been made sad in my exercise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To be exercised indeed among evil men Thou hast set me, but too much they have risen up, beyond my powers: calm Thou one troubled, stretch forth a hand to one sinking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For they have brought down upon me iniquity, and in anger they were shadowing me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard of waves and winds: one as it were humbled they were insulting, and he was praying: on every side against him with the roar of insult they were raging, but he within was calling upon Him whom they did not see.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this man being troubled and made sad was praying, his eye being disturbed as it were on account of anger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the anger of a brother if it shall have been inveterate is then hatred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Anger doth trouble the eye, hatred doth quench it: anger is a straw, hatred is a beam.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes thou hatest and chidest an angry man: in thee is hatred, in him whom thou chidest anger: with reason to thee is said, “Cast out first the beam from thine own eye, and so thou shalt see to cast out the straw from thy brother’s eye.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that ye may know how much difference there is between anger and hatred: day by day men are angry with their sons, show me them that hate their sons!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man being troubled was praying even when made sad, wrestling against all revilings of all revilers; not in order that he might conquer any one of them by giving back reviling, but that he might not hate any one of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence he prayeth, hence asketh: “From the voice of the enemy and from the tribulation of the sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My heart hath been troubled in me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the same as elsewhere hath been said, “Mine eye because of anger hath been troubled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if eye hath been troubled, what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And fear of death hath fallen upon me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our life is love: if life is love, death is hatred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When a man hath begun to fear lest he should hate him that he was loving, it is death he is fearing; and a sharper death, and a more inward death, whereby soul is killed, not body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst mind a man raging against thee; what was he to do, against whom thine own Lord had given thee security, saying, “Fear not them that kill the body”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He by raging killeth body, thou by keeping hatred hast killed soul; and he the body of another, thou thine own soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fear,” therefore, “of death hath fallen upon me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fearfulness and trembling have come upon me, and darkness hath covered me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have said,” “He that hateth his brother, is in darkness until now.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If love is light, hatred is darkness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what saith to himself one set in that weakness and troubled in that exercise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who shall give me wings as to a dove, and I shall fly and shall rest?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Either for death he was wishing, or for solitude he was longing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So long, he saith, as this is the work with me, as this command is given me, that I should love enemies, the revilings of these men, increasing and shadowing me, do derange mine eye, perturb my sight, penetrate my heart, slay my soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I could wish to depart, but weak I am, lest by abiding I should add sins to sins: or at least may I be separated for a little space from mankind, lest my wound suffer from frequent blows, in order that when it hath been made whole it may be brought back to the exercise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is what takes place, brethren, and there ariseth ofttimes in the mind of the servant of God a longing for solitude, for no other reason than because of the multitude of tribulations and scandals, and he saith, “Who shall give me wings?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth he find himself without wings, or rather with bound wings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they are wanting, be they given; if bound, be they loosed; because even he that looseth a bird’s wings, either giveth, or giveth back to it its wings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it had not as though its own them, wherewith it could not fly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bound wings make a burden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who,” he saith, “shall give me wings as to a dove, and I shall fly and shall rest?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall rest, where?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have said there are two senses here: either, as saith the Apostle, “To be dissolved and to be with Christ, for it is by far the best thing.”…Even he that amended cannot be, is thine, either by the fellowship of the human race, or ofttimes by Church Communion; he is within, what wilt thou do? whither wilt go? whither separate thyself, in order that these things thou mayest not suffer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But go to him, speak, exhort, coax, threaten, reprove.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have done all things, whatever powers I had I have expended and have drained, nothing I see have I prevailed; all my labour hath been spent out, sorrow hath remained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then shall my heart rest from such men, except I say, “Who shall give me wings?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As to a dove,” however, not as to a raven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A dove seeketh a flying away from troubles, but she loseth not love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a dove as a type of love is set forth, and in her the plaint is loved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing is so fond of plaints as a dove: day and night she complaineth, as though she were set here where she ought to complain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then saith this lover?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Revilings of men to bear I am unable, they roar, with frenzy are carried away, are inflamed with indignation, in anger they shadow me; to do good to them I am unable; O that I might rest somewhere, being separated from them in body, not in love; lest in me there should be troubled love itself: with my words and my speech no good can I do them, by praying for them perchance I shall do good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These words men say, but ofttimes they are so bound, that to fly they are not able.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For perchance they are not bound with any birdlime, but are bound by duty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if they are bound with care and duty, and to leave it are unable, let them say, “I was wishing to be dissolved and to be with Christ, for it is by far the best thing: to abide in the flesh is necessary because of you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A dove bound back by affection, not by cupidity, was not able to fly away because of duty to be fulfilled, not because of little merit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless a longing in heart must needs be; nor doth any man suffer this longing, but he that hath begun to walk in that narrow way: in order that he may know that there are not wanting to the Church persecutions, even in this time, when a calm is seen in the Church, at least with respect to those persecutions which our Martyrs have suffered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are not wanting persecutions, because a true saying is this, “All that will godly to live in Christ, shall suffer persecution.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold I have gone afar fleeing, and have abode in the desert” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what desert?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherever thou shalt be, there will gather them together other men, the desert with thee they will seek, will attach themselves to thy life, thou canst not thrust back the society of brethren: there are mingled with thee also evil men; still exercise is thy due portion, “Behold I have gone afar, and have abode in the desert.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what desert?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is perchance in the conscience, whither no man entereth, where no one is with thee, where thou art and God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if in the desert, in any place, what wilt thou do with men gathering themselves together?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou wilt not be able to be separated from mankind, so long as among men thou livest.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I was looking for him that should save me from weakness of mind and tempest” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sea there is, tempest there is: nothing for thee remaineth but to cry out, “Lord, I perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him stretch forth hand, who doth the waves tread fearlessly, let Him relieve thy dread, let Him confirm in Himself thy security, let Him speak to thee within, and say to thee, “Give heed to Me, what I have borne:” an evil brother perchance thou art suffering, or an enemy without art suffering; which of these have I not suffered?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There roared without Jews, within a disciple was betraying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There rageth therefore tempest, but He doth save men from weakness of mind, and tempest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perchance thy ship is being troubled, because He in thee is sleeping.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sea was raging, the bark wherein the disciples were sailing was being tossed; but Christ was sleeping: at length it was seen by them that among them was sleeping the Ruler and Creator of winds; they drew near and awoke Christ; He commanded the winds, and there was a great calm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason then perchance thy heart is troubled, because thou hast forgotten Him on whom thou hast believed: beyond endurance thou art suffering, because it hath not come into thy mind what for thee Christ hath borne.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If unto thy mind cometh not Christ, He sleepeth: awake Christ, recall faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For then in thee Christ is sleeping, if thou hast forgotten the sufferings of Christ: then in thee Christ is watching, if thou hast remembered the sufferings of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when with full heart thou shalt have considered what He hath suffered, wilt not thou too with equanimity endure? and perchance rejoicing, because thou hast been found in some likeness of the sufferings of thy King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore on these things thinking thou hast begun to be comforted and to rejoice, He hath arisen, He hath commanded the winds; therefore there is a great calm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I was looking for Him that should save me from weakness of mind and tempest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sink, O Lord, and divide the tongues of them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is referring to men troubling him and shadowing him, and he hath wished this thing not of anger, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that have wickedly lifted up themselves, for them it is expedient that they be sunk.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that have wickedly conspired, it is expedient for them that their tongues should be divided: to good let them consent, and let their tongues agree together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if to one purpose there were a whispering against me, he saith, all mine enemies, let them lose their “one purpose” in evil, divided be the tongues of them, let them not with themselves agree together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sink, O Lord, and divide the tongues of them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore “sink”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because themselves they have lifted up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore “divide”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because for an evil thing they have united.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Recollect that tower of proud men made after the deluge: what said the proud men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest we perish in a deluge, let us make a lofty tower.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In pride they were thinking themselves to be fortified, they builded up a lofty tower, and the Lord divided the tongues of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then they began not to understand one another; hence arose the beginning of many tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For before, one tongue there was: but one tongue for men agreeing was good, one tongue for humble men was good: but when that gathering together did into a union of pride fall headlong, God spared them; even though He divided the tongues, lest by understanding one another they should make a destructive unity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through proud men, divided were the tongues; through humble Apostles, united were the tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Spirit of pride dispersed tongues, Spirit Holy united tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when the Holy Spirit came upon the disciples, with the tongues of all men they spake, by all men they were understood: tongues dispersed, into one were united.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore if still they rage and are Gentiles, it is expedient for them divided to have their tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They would have one tongue; let them come to the Church; because even among the diversity of tongues of flesh, one is the tongue in faith of heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I have seen iniquity and contradiction in the city.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason this man was seeking the desert, for he saw iniquity and contradiction in the city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a certain city turbulent: the same it was that was building a tower, the same was confounded and called Babylon, the same through innumerable nations dispersed: thence is gathered the Church into the desert of a good conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he saw contradiction in the city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Christ cometh.”—“What Christ?” thou contradictest.—“Son of God.”—“And hath God a Son?” thou contradictest.—“He was born of a virgin, suffered, rose again.”—“And whence is it possible for this to be done?” thou contradictest.—Give heed at least to the glory of the Cross itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now on the brow of kings that Cross hath been fixed, over which enemies insulted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The effect hath proved the virtue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath subdued the world, not with steel, but with wood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The wood of the Cross deserving of insults hath seemed to enemies, and before the wood itself standing they were wagging the head, and saying, “If Son of God He is, let Him come down from the Cross.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was stretching forth His hands to a people unbelieving and contradicting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if just he is that of faith liveth, unjust he is that hath not faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By that which here he saith “iniquity,” I understand unbelief.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord therefore was seeing in the city iniquity and contradiction, and was stretching forth His hands to a people unbelieving and contradicting: and nevertheless waiting for these same, He was saying, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now indeed there rage the remnant of that city, even now they contradict.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the brows of all men now He is stretching forth hands to the remnant unbelieving and contradicting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Day and night there will compass it upon the walls thereof iniquity, and labour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon the walls thereof;” upon the fortifications thereof, holding as it were the heads thereof, the noble men thereof.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If that noble man were a Christian, not one would remain a pagan!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Oft-times men say, “no one would remain a pagan, if he were a Christian.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ofttimes men say, “If he too were made a Christian, who would remain a pagan?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because therefore not yet they are made Christians, as if walls they are of that city unbelieving and contradicting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How long shall these walls stand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not always shall they stand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Ark is going around the walls of Jericho: there shall come a time at the seventh going round of the Ark, when all the walls of the city unbelieving and contradicting shall fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Until it come to pass, this man is being troubled in his exercise; and enduring the remains of men contradicting, he would choose wings for flying away, would choose the rest of the desert.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea let him continue amid men contradicting, let him endure menaces, drink revilings, and look for Him that will save him from weakness of mind and tempest: let him look upon the Head, the pattern for his life, let him be made calm in hope, even if he is troubled in fact.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Day and night there will compass it upon the walls thereof iniquity; and labour in the midst thereof and injustice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And for this reason labour is there, because iniquity is there: because injustice is there, therefore also labour is there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let them hear him stretching forth hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come unto Me, all ye that labour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye cry, ye contradict, ye revile: He on the contrary, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour,” in your pride, and ye shall rest in My humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Learn of Me,” He saith, “for meek I am and humble in heart, and ye shall find rest unto your souls.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whence do they labour, but because they are not meek and humble in heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God humble was made, let man blush to be proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There hath not failed from the streets thereof usury and deceit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Usury and deceit are not hidden at least, because they are evil things, but in public they rage.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that in his house doth any evil thing, however for his evil thing doth blush: “In the streets thereof usury and deceit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Money-lending even hath a profession, Money-lending also is called a science; a corporation is spoken of, a corporation as if necessary to the state, and of its profession it payeth revenue; so entirely indeed in the streets is that which should have been hidden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is also another usury worse, when thou forgivest not that which to thee is owed; and the eye is disturbed in that verse of the prayer, “Forgive us our debts—as we too forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what there wilt thou do, when thou art going to pray, and coming to that same verse?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An insulting word thou hast heard: thou wouldest exact the punishment of condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do but consent to exact just so much as thou hast given, thou usurer of injuries!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With the fist thou hast been smitten, slaying thou seekest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How wilt thou go to prayer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou shalt have left praying, which way wilt thou come round unto the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold thou wilt say: “Our Father which art in heaven, hallowed be Thy Name, Thy kingdom come, Thy will be done, as in heaven so on earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt say, “Our daily bread give us to-day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt come to, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even in that evil city let there abound these usuries; let them not enter the walls where the breast is smitten!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wilt thou do? because there thou and that verse are in the midst?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Petitions for thee hath a heavenly Lawyer composed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that knew what used there to be done, said to thee, “Otherwise thou shalt not obtain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, that if ye shall have forgiven men sins, they shall be forgiven you; but if ye shall not have forgiven sins unto men, neither will your Father forgive you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who saith this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that knoweth what there is being done, in the place whereat thou art standing to make request.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how Himself hath willed to be thy Advocate; Himself thy Counsellor, Himself the Assessor of the Father, Himself thy Judge hath said, “Otherwise thou shalt not receive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wilt thou do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt not receive, unless thou shalt speak; wilt not receive if falsely thou shalt speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore either thou must do and speak, or else what thou askest thou wilt not earn; because they that this do not do, are in the midst of those evil usuries.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be they engaged therein, that yet do idols either adore or desire: do not thou, O people of God, do not thou, O people of Christ, do not thou the Body of Him the Head!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give heed to the bond of thy peace, give heed to the promise of thy life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what doth it profit thee, that thou exactest for injuries which thou hast endured? doth vengeance refresh thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, over the evil of another shalt thou rejoice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast suffered evil; pardon thou; be not ye two.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if an enemy had upbraided me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And indeed above he was “troubled in his exercise” by the voice of the enemy and by the tribulation of the sinner, perhaps being placed in that city, that proud city that was building a tower, which was “sunk,” that divided might be the tongues: give heed to his inward groaning because of perils from false brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if an enemy had upbraided me, I would have undergone it assuredly, and if he that did hate me had over me spoken great words,” that is, through pride had on me trampled, did magnify himself above me, did threaten me all in his power: “I would hide myself assuredly from him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From him that is abroad, thou wouldest hide thyself where?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amid those that are within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now see whether anything else remaineth, but that thou seek solitude.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But thou,” he saith, “man of one mind, my guide and my friend” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perchance sometimes good counsel thou hast given, perchance sometimes thou hast gone before me, and some wholesome advice thou hast given me: in the Church of God together we have been.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But thou,…that together with me didst take sweet morsels” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the sweet morsels?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not all they that are present know: but let them not be soured that do know, in order that they may be able to say to them that as yet know not: “Taste ye and see, how sweet is the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the House of God we have walked with consent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then dissension?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou that wast within, hast become one without.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath walked with me in the House of God with consent: another house hath he set up against the House of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore hath that been forsaken, wherein we have walked with consent? wherefore hath that been deserted, wherein together we did take sweet morsels?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let there come death upon them, and let them go down unto Hell living” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How hath he cited and hath made us call to mind that first beginning of schism, when in that first people of the Jews certain proud men separated themselves, and would without have sacrificed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A new death upon them came: the earth opened herself, and swallowed them up alive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let there come,” he saith, “death upon them, and let them go down into Hell living.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “living”? knowing that they are perishing, and yet perishing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear of living men perishing and being swallowed up in a gulf of the earth, that is, being swallowed up in the voraciousness of earthly desires.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou sayest to a man, What aileth thee, brother?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren we are, one God we invoke, in one Christ we believe, one Gospel we hear, one Psalm we sing, one Amen we respond, one Hallelujah we sound, one Easter we celebrate: why art thou without and I am within?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ofttimes one straitened, and perceiving how true are the charges which are made, saith, May God requite our ancestors!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore alive he perisheth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the next place thou continuest and thus givest warning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At least let the evil of separation stand alone, why dost thou adjoin thereto that of rebaptism?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Acknowledge in me what thou hast; and if thou hatest me, spare thou Christ in me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this evil thing doth frequently and very greatly displease them.…Because they themselves have the Scriptures in their hands, and know well by daily reading how the Church Catholic through the whole world is so spread, that in a word all contradiction is void; and that there cannot be found any support for their schism they know well: therefore unto the lower places living they go down, because the evil which they do, they know evil to be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the former a fire of divine indignation consumed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For being inflamed with desire of strife, from their evil leaders they would not depart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There came upon fire a fire, upon the heat of dissension the heat of consuming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For naughtiness is in their lodgings, in the midst of them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In their lodgings,” wherein they tarry and pass away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For here they are not alway to be: and nevertheless in defence of a temporal animosity they are fighting so fiercely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In their lodgings is iniquity; in the midst of them is iniquity:” no part of them is so near the middle of them as their heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore to the Lord I have cried out” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Body of Christ and the oneness of Christ in anguish, in weariness, in uneasiness, in the tribulation of its exercise, that One Man, Oneness in One Body set, when He was wearying His soul in crying out from the ends of the earth; saith, “From the ends of the earth to Thee I have cried out, when My heart was being vexed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself one, but a oneness that One! and Himself one, not in one place one, but from the ends of the earth is crying as one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How from the ends of the earth should there cry one, except there were one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I to the Lord have cried out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rightly do thou cry out to the Lord, cry not to Donatus: lest for thee he be instead of the Lord a lord, that under the Lord would not be a fellow-servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In evening, in morning, at noon-day I will recount and will tell forth, and He shall hearken to my voice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou proclaim glad tidings, keep not secret that which thou hast received, “in evening” of things gone by, “in morning” of things to be, at “noonday” of things ever to be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, to that which he saith “in evening” belongeth that which he recounteth: to that which he saith, “in morning,” belongeth that which he telleth forth: to that which he saith “at noon-day,” belongeth that wherein his voice is hearkened to.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the end is at noon-day; that is to say, whence there is no going down unto setting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For at noon-day there is light full high, the splendour of wisdom, the fervour of love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In evening and in morning and at noon-day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In evening,” the Lord on the Cross; “in morning,” in Resurrection; “at noon-day,” in Ascension.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will recount in evening the patience of Him dying, I will tell forth in morning the life of Him rising, I will pray that He hearken at noon-day sitting at the right hand of the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shall hearken to my voice, That intercedeth for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great is the security of this man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great the consolation, how great the refuge “from weakness of mind and tempest,” against evil men, against ungodly men both without and within, and in the case of those that are without though they had been within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, my Brethren, those that in the very congregation of these walls ye see to be rebellious men, proud, seeking their own, lifted up; not having a zeal for God that is chaste, sound, quiet, but ascribing to themselves much; ready for dissension, but not finding opportunity; are the very chaff of the Lord’s floor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From hence these few men the wind of pride hath dislodged: the whole floor will not fly, save when He at the last shall winnow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what shall we do, save with this man sing, with this man pray, with this man mourn and say securely, “He shall redeem in peace my soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against them that love not peace: “in peace He shall redeem my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because with those that hated peace I was peace-making.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall redeem in peace my soul, from those that draw near to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from those that are afar from me, it is an easy case: not so soon doth he deceive me that saith, Come, pray to an idol: he is very far from me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Art thou a Christian?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Christian, he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of a neighbouring place he is my adversary, he is at hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall redeem in peace my soul, from those that draw near to me: for in many things they were with me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore have I said, “draw near to me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “in many things they were with me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this verse two propositions occur.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In many things they were with me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Baptism we had both of us, in that they were with me: the Gospel we both read, they were in that with me: the festivals of martyrs we celebrated, they were there with me: Easter’s solemnity we attended, they were there with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But not entirely with me: in schism not with me, in heresy not with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In many things with me, in few things not with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in these few things wherein not with me, there is no profit to them of the many things wherein they were with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see, brethren, how many things hath recounted the Apostle Paul: one thing, he hath said, if it shall have been wanting, in vain are those things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If with the tongues of men and of angels I shall speak,” he saith, “if I have all prophecy, and all faith, and all knowledge; if mountains I shall remove, if I shall bestow all my goods upon the poor, if I shall deliver my body even so that it be burned.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many things he hath enumerated!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To all these many things let there be wanting one thing, charity; the former in number are more, the latter in weight is greater.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore in all Sacraments they are with me, in one charity not with me: “In many things they were with me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, by a different expression: “For in many things they were with me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that themselves have separated from me, with me they were, not in few things, but in many things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For throughout the whole world few are the grains, many are the chaffs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In chaff with me they were, in wheat with me they were not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the chaff is nearly related to the wheat, from one seed it goeth forth, in one field is rooted, with one rain is nourished, the same reaper it suffereth, the same threshing sustaineth, the same winnowing awaiteth, but not into one barn entereth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God will hear me, and He shall humble them That is before ages” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they rely on some leader or other of theirs that hath begun but yesterday.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall humble them That is before ages.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even if with reference to time Christ is of Mary the Virgin, nevertheless before ages: “In the beginning He is the Word and the Word with God, and the Word God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall humble them That is before ages.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to them is no changing:” of them I “speak to whom is no changing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knew of some to persevere, and in the perseverance of their own wickedness to die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we see them, and to them is no changing: they that die in that same perverseness, in that same schism, to them is no changing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God shall humble them, shall humble them in damnation, because they are exalted in dissension.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To them is no changing, because they are not changed for the better, but for the worse: neither while they are here, nor in the resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all we shall rise again, but not all shall be changed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “’To them is no changing: and they have not feared God.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He stretcheth forth His hand in requiting” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have polluted His Testament.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Read the testament which they have polluted: “In thy seed shall be blessed all nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou against these words of the Testator sayest what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Africa of holy Donatus hath alone deserved this grace, in him hath remained the Church of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say at least the Church of Donatus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore addest thou, of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of whom it is said, “In thy seed shall be blessed all nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After Donatus wilt thou go?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Set aside Christ, and then secede.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See therefore what followeth: “They have polluted His Testament.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Testament?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To Abraham have been spoken the promises, and to his seed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle saith, “Nevertheless, a man’s testament confirmed no one maketh void, or super-addeth to: to Abraham have been spoken the promises, and to his seed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith not, And to seeds, as if in many; but as if in one, And to thy Seed, which is Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this Christ, therefore, what Testament hath been promised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In thy seed shall be blessed all nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou that hast given up the unity of all nations, and in a part hast remained, hast polluted His Testament.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His heart hath drawn near” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of whom do we understand it, except of Him, by the anger of whom they have been divided?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How “hath his heart drawn near”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such sort, that we may understand His will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by heretics hath been vindicated the Catholic Church, and by those that think evil have been proved those that think well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many things lay hid in the Scriptures: and when heretics had been cut off, with questions they troubled the Church of God: then those things were opened which lay hid, and the will of God was understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is said in another Psalm, “In order that they might be excluded that have been proved with silver.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For let them be excluded, He hath said, let them come forth, let them appear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence even in silver-working men are called “excluders,” that is, pressers out of form from the sort of confusion of the lump.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore many men that could understand and expound the Scriptures very excellently, were hidden among the people of God: but they did not declare the solution of difficult questions, when no reviler again urged them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For was the Trinity perfectly treated of before the Arians snarled thereat?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was repentance perfectly treated of before the Novatians opposed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So not perfectly of Baptism was it treated, before rebaptizers removed outside contradicted; nor of the very oneness of Christ were the doctrines clearly stated which have been stated, save after that this separation began to press upon the weak: in order that they that knew how to treat of and solve these questions (lest the weak should perish vexed with the questions of the ungodly), by their discourses and disputations should bring out unto open day the dark things of the Law.…This obscure sense see in what manner the Apostle bringeth out into light; “It is needful,” he saith, “that also heresies there be, in order that men proved may be made manifest among you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “men proved”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Proved with silver, proved with the word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “may be made manifest”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May be brought out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because of heretics.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So therefore these also “have been divided because of the anger of His countenance, and His heart hath drawn near.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His discourses have been softened above oil, and themselves are darts” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For certain things in the Scriptures were seeming hard, while they were obscure; when explained, they have been softened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even the first heresy in the disciples of Christ, as it were from the hardness of His discourse arose.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when He said, “Except a man shall have eaten My flesh and shall have drunk My blood, he shall not have life in himself:” they, not understanding, said to one another, “Hard is this discourse, who can hear it?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Saying that, “Hard is this discourse,” they separated from Him: He remained with the others, the twelve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they had intimated to Him, that by His discourse they had been scandalized, “Will ye also,” He saith, “choose to go?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then Peter: “Thou hast the Word of life eternal: to whom shall we go?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend, we beseech you, and ye little ones learn godliness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did Peter by any means at that time understand the secret of that discourse of the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet he understood: but that good were the words which he understood not, godly he believed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore if hard is a discourse, and not yet is understood, be it hard to an ungodly man, but to thee be it by godliness softened: for whenever it is solved, it both will become for thee oil, and even unto the bones it will penetrate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore, just as Peter, after their having been scandalized by the hardness, as they thought, of the discourse of the Lord, even then said, “to whom shall we go?” so he hath added, “Cast upon the Lord thy care, and He shall Himself nourish thee up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A little one thou art, not yet thou understandest the secret things of words: perchance from thee the bread is hidden, and as yet with milk thou must be fed: be not angry with the breasts: they will make thee fit for the table, for which now little fitted thou art.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold by the division of heretics many hard things have been softened: His discourses that were hard have been softened above oil, and they are themselves darts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have armed men preaching the Gospel: and the very discourses are aimed at the breast of every one that heareth, by men instant in season and out of season: by those discourses, by those words, as though by arrows, hearts of men unto the love of peace are smitten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hard they were, and soft they have been made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Being softened they have not lost their virtue, but into darts have been converted.…Upon the Lord cast thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold thou wilt cast thyself upon the Lord, let no one put himself in the place of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Cast upon the Lord thy care.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to the others what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, O God, shall bring them down unto the pit of corruption” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The pit of corruption is the darkness of sinking under.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When blind leadeth blind, they both fall into a ditch.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God bringeth them down into the pit of corruption, not because He is the author of their own guilt, but because He is Himself the judge of their iniquities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God hath delivered them unto the desires of their heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they have loved darkness, and not light; they have loved blindness, and not seeing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For behold the Lord Jesus hath shone out to the whole world, let them sing in unity with the whole world: “For there is not one that can hide himself from the heat of Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they passing over from the whole to a part, from the body to a wound, from life to a limb cut off, shall meet with what, but going into the pit of corruption?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Men of bloods and of deceitfulness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men of bloods, because of slayings he calleth them: and O that they were corporal and not spiritual slayings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For blood from the flesh going forth, is seen and shuddered at: who seeth the blood of the heart in a man rebaptized?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those deaths require other eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although even about these visible deaths Circumcelliones armed everywhere remain not quiet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if we think of these visible deaths, there are men of bloods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give heed to the armed man, whether he is a man of peace and not of blood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If at least a club only he were to carry, well; but he carrieth a sling, carrieth an axe, carrieth stones, carrieth lances; and carrying these weapons, wherever they may they scour, for the blood of innocent men they thirst.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore even with regard to these visible deaths there are men of bloods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even of them let us say, O that such deaths alone they perpetrated, and souls they slew not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These that are men of bloods and of deceit, let them not suppose that we thus wrongly understand men of bloods, of them that kill souls: they themselves of their Maximianists have so understood it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when they condemned them, in the very sentence of their Council they have set down these words: “Swift are the feet of them to shed the blood” (of the proclaimers), “tribulation and calamity are in the ways of them, and the way of peace they have not known.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This of the Maximianists they have said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I ask of them, when have the Maximianists shed the body’s blood; not because they too would not shed, if there were so great a multitude as could shed, but because of the fear in their minority rather they have suffered somewhat from others, than have themselves at any time done any such thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore I question the Donatist and say: In thy Council thou hast set down of the Maximianists, “Swift are the feet of them to shed blood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Show me one of whom the Maximianists have hurt so much as a finger!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What other thing to me is he to answer, than that which I say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that have separated themselves from unity, and who slay souls by leading astray, spiritually, not carnally, do shed blood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Very well thou hast expounded, but in thy exposition acknowledge their own deeds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Men of bloods and of deceitfulness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In guile is deceitfulness, in dissimulation, in seduction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore of those very men that have been divided because of the anger of His countenance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are themselves men of bloods and of deceit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But of them he saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall not halve their days.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “They shall not halve their days”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They shall not make progress as much as they think: within the time which they expect, they shall perish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he is that partridge, whereof hath been said, “In the half of his days they shall leave him, and in his last days he shall be an unwise one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They make progress, but for a time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what saith the Apostle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But evil men and seducers shall make progress for the worse, themselves erring, and other men into error driving.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “a blind man leading a blind man, together into a ditch they fall.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deservedly they fall “into the pit of corruption.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They shall make progress for the worse: not however for long.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a little before he hath said, “But further they shall not make progress:” that is, “shall not halve their days.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Apostle proceed and tell wherefore: “For the madness of them shall be manifest to all men, as also was that of the others.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I in Thee will hope, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But deservedly they shall not halve their days, because in man they have hoped.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I from days temporal have reached unto day eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because in Thee I have hoped, O Lord.
